name: public_health_predictive_queue_begins_southport_car_fair
premise: A Public Health Infrastructure response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving public health predictive queue begins southport car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Soren Kim
agents.identity: I am Soren Kim, accountable for a defensible call in Public Health Infrastructure when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Arjun Hughes
agents.identity: I am Arjun Hughes, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Public Health Infrastructure.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Soren Kim must decide under care vs fairness. What does Soren Kim do?
